description: Matt Zenon, an industrial radiographer, was accidentally exposed to an unshielded Iridium-192 source (estimated whole-body dose: 0.8 Gy). He is hemodynamically stable but reports mild nausea and anxiety. As the Occupational Health Physician, initiate the Acute Radiation Syndrome (ARS) triage protocol in GNU Health by documenting the radiation exposure diagnosis (ICD-10 W90 or Z57.1) as an active condition and recording a clinical evaluation documenting an elevated heart rate (>= 80 bpm) due to anxiety. Order at least two baseline laboratory studies, including a CBC for baseline lymphocyte count and a metabolic panel. Prescribe a prophylactic antiemetic (Ondansetron, Metoclopramide, or Promethazine) and schedule a follow-up appointment between 2 and 7 days from today for serial lymphocyte tracking. Login: admin / gnusolidario.
